calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Judgment On
Grounds Of Extrinsic Fraud, Newly Discovered Evidence,
Perjured Testimony, And Misconduct By Opposing Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Prior to Open Court, Mr. Seidler appears via Zoom. The Court received a late notice to contest the Court's tentative ruling. At the instruction of the Court, the matter is not called. The Court's tentative ruling is the final ruling of the court.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Joseph Seidler's Motion For Relief From Judgment On Grounds Of Extrinsic Fraud, Newly Discovered Evidence, Perjured Testimony, And Misconduct By Opposing Counsel is DENIED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Judgment On
Grounds Of Extrinsic Fraud, Newly Discovered Evidence,
Perjured Testimony, And Misconduct By Opposing Counsel
Off Calendar May-01-2026 Continued To Jun-05-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOSEPH SEIDLER, GARY CATTICH's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Counsel Elizabeth A. Sperling and Glaser Weil Fink Howard Jordan & Shapiro LLP's unopposed Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Petitioner and Cross-Respondent Joseph A. Seidler is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, moving parties Elizabeth A. Sperling and Glaser Weil Fink Howard Jordan & Shapiro LLP will be relieved as counsel for Petitioner and Cross-Respondent Joseph A. Seidler as provided in the executed MC-053.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

APPELLANT JOSEPH SEIDLER's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Plaintiff counsel Elizabeth A. Sperling and Glaser Weil Fink Howard Jordan Shapiro LLP's unopposed motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Defendant Damion Davis is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

The trial court loses jurisdiction over most aspects of a case when, as here, an appeal is taken. Moving counsel has not shown acting on the motion to be relieved is within the scope of matters over which this court still has jurisdiction. Moving counsel has filed a motion to be relieved in the Court of Appeal. If that motion is granted, Moving Counsel should file notice of that order in this docket.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award Against Plaintiff/Respondent Gary Cattich; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Timothy N. Will
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Gary Cattich, Plaintiff

Plaintiff advises the Court his current counsel, Mark C. Thomas, unexpectedly passed away two weeks ago.

Not Contested. Not argued/ The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award Against Plaintiff/Respondent Gary Cattich.

Defendant's petition to confirm contractual arbitration award is granted; no timely opposition filed.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2024 Off Calendar

PLAINTIFF'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD
Off Calendar Aug-29-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
MARK C. THOMAS, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF MARK C. THOMAS, APC 415-986-1338 mark@mctlawoffice.com
Attorney for Plaintiffs Joseph Seidler and Gary Cattich
No appearance by Defendant's counsel Tim Will, Esq.,

Prior to the start of today's hearing, Mr. Will sent an email to the court indicating that there may be a possible electrical outage in his area and this would affect his ability to appea by Zoom. The clerk emailed counsel with the information required to join ZOOM by telephone. During the clerks' check in process, multiple attempts to get an appearance from telephone number ending 4576 were made. No response/appearance was stated. An email was received at 10:38 a.m. by Mr. Will indicating that he was present on ZOOM at the number ending 4576, but that the court was unable to hear him. It should be noted that the clerk did provide access to allow Mr. Will to speak.

Hearing is held. Discussed, not argued. Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD is taken under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC's Motion By Respondent And Cross-Petitioner Wells Fargo Clearing Services Llc'S On Its Petition To Confirm Finra Arbitration Award Against Joseph Seidler.

Continued to August 29, 2024. Wells Fargo's reply brief filed on August 9, 2024, refers to Seidler's opposition to Wells Fargo's petition to confirm. That opposition brief (and the Thomas declaration) purportedly explain the basis to deny confirmation and grant vacation of the arbitration award under Armendariz v Foundation Health Psychcare Services Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Seidler attempted to file the opposition on August 6, 2024, but the clerk rejected it. The brief is therefore not on file with the court. The motion to vacate and motion to confirm are continued to August 29, 2024, to give Seidler an opportunity to file the opposition. The opposition shall be filed with the court by August 19, 2024. The parties are reminded of local rule 2.7B requiring courtesy copies of all papers. Please provide courtesy copies of all filings with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date.

Judge: Curtis C. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
